title: Implement Audio Calibration & Test Mode
description:
# Implement Audio Calibration & Test Mode

## 📝 User Story / Description
To safely and accurately tune visualizer settings (especially the VFD display response), the user requires a closed-loop test signal generator built into the application. This eliminates reliance on random music playback during development.

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A synthetic `OscillatorNode` is created in Web Audio API.
- [ ] A sweep algorithm scales through frequencies (20Hz to 15kHz).
- [ ] A periodic loud burst simulates rhythmic bass beats.
- [ ] The test signal is audible through system speakers.
- [ ] A toggle switch exists in the UI Settings menu to activate it.

## 🛠️ Technical Notes
- Add `testModeActive` state variable.
- Implement `testOscillator` generator in `audio.ts` which routes into both the standard analyser nodes and `audioCtx.destination` to ensure the audio is audible on the host speakers.

## 🛡️ Rigor & Verification Boundary
- [ ] The visualizer reacts to the synthetic test signal identically to live audio capture.

- **Quality Debt Exemptions:**
  - `complexity_limit: false`
  - `doc_drift: false`
  - `dry_candidate: false`

### 📝 Git Commit Details
```
Implement Audible Calibration Test Signal

Reasoning:
The user requested a way to cleanly verify the application's response to bass hits, sweeping frequencies, and general music playback without relying on unpredictable external music inputs. This is specifically needed to calibrate the VFD and Equalizer styles. Providing a closed-loop test generator creates a deterministic way to tune settings.

Changes:
- Implemented `toggleCalibrationMode` in `src/audio.ts` utilizing a synthetic `OscillatorNode` and `GainNode`.
- Created an automated 5-second logarithmic frequency sweep (20Hz -> 15kHz) and a 120-BPM volume pulsing envelope.
- Routed the `GainNode` directly to both the internal `setupAnalyserNodes` engine and `audioCtx.destination` for audible output.
- Hooked the mode into a new "Calibration Signal" toggle in the Settings Menu (`visualizer/index.html` and `src/main.ts`), managing state globally in `src/state.ts`.

— *Nomos Engine (v.v1.0.1-21-g7e7aa96)*
```

### 📖 Walkthrough / Specs Checklist
# Audio Calibration & Test Signal Mode

## Acceptance Criteria Coverage
- Created an `OscillatorNode` and `GainNode` synthetic test signal generator in `src/audio.ts` via the `toggleCalibrationMode` function.
- Implemented a 5-second logarithmic loop that sweeps from 20Hz to 15kHz, simulating real musical frequencies.
- Implemented a 120-BPM volume pulsing envelope on the `GainNode` to simulate percussive bass beats.
- Connected the `GainNode` to the `audioCtx.destination` to ensure the test signal plays audibly through the desktop speakers.
- Added a "Calibration Signal" toggle in the Music HUD Settings in `visualizer/index.html` which natively binds to the mode and refreshes the application on exit to cleanly restore microphone tracking.

## Verification
- Verified compilation via TypeScript.
- The UI triggers the calibration logic without error. The internal audio graph correctly unhooks from `getUserMedia` loopbacks and directly feeds the `OscillatorNode` to the visualizer logic, perfectly enabling precise VFD and EQ testing.
